sample.assertion: Accuracy = proportion of predictions that matched the correct answer
Classification task defined as assigning input to one of a fixed set of categories
Correct predictions and incorrect predictions are the two raw building-block counts
Accuracy = correct ÷ total; result between 0 and 1
Spam filter on 95/100 not-spam emails scores 95% by doing nothing useful
Dummy baseline: score by always predicting most common category
LLM outputs probability distribution over vocabulary; temperature controls peakedness (topics 7.4/7.5)
Spam filter worked example: 85 correct out of 100 = 0.85 (85%)
Medical screening: disease affecting 1 in 1000 → 99.9% accuracy labelling everyone disease-free
Do not measure accuracy on training data — use a held-out test set

faithfulness.notes: All 10 sampled assertions trace directly to corpus. No hallucinated or externally imported material detected.

beginner_readability.notes: 'Classification task', 'ground truth', 'imbalanced', 'dummy baseline', 'correct predictions', and 'incorrect predictions' all defined at first use using bold-term/plain-meaning shape. Concrete spam/not-spam examples precede all abstract formulations. No hedging phrases. Sentences are short and active throughout. Learner addressed as 'you'.
